content.text: A hybrid AI app has two distinct processing layers: ML Kit runs on-device and always returns something immediately; Claude runs in the cloud and may not be reachable. When the device is offline, the cloud layer must degrade gracefully without losing the user's request. The offline queue pattern covers this: ML Kit answers immediately with what it can, the cloud portion of the request is persisted to a local Room database, and WorkManager drains the queue when connectivity is restored.
Security constraint:

Queued requests must not contain the Claude API key. The queue stores the user's text plus a session token. The backend proxy, which holds the Claude API key, is called only when the device is online. Sensitive queue entries (health data, personal notes) should be encrypted using SQLCipher or an encrypted Room passphrase — unencrypted Room databases are readable by anyone with USB debugging or a device backup.
State Machine Overview

attrs.alt: State machine: online path (ML Kit → proxy → Claude → cache) vs offline path (ML Kit partial result → Room queue → WorkManager drain on reconnect)
attrs.title: Offline Queue and Cloud Sync State Machine

content.text: Room Schema: Pending Requests and Result Cache
Two tables underpin the system:
pending_ai_requests
holds cloud queries that have not yet been dispatched;
cached_results
holds successful responses so repeated queries can be served instantly offline. Entries in
pending_ai_requests
carry a TTL: queries older than your configured window are not worth processing (the user's context has changed) and are dropped instead of retried.
Database Setup
WorkManager: Drain Worker

The drain worker runs when connectivity is available. It fetches live entries, calls the proxy for each, and removes successfully processed entries. Entries that fail increment their retry counter; WorkManager retries the entire worker using exponential backoff. An entry that accumulates too many retries is removed — the session token may have expired and a fresh login is required.
Repository: Optimistic UI with Queue Fallback

The repository abstracts the routing logic. It always returns an ML Kit result immediately as the optimistic answer, then either streams the Claude response (online) or enqueues the request and schedules the drain worker (offline). The cache is checked before the queue — a recent cached response is returned immediately rather than enqueueing a duplicate.
ViewModel: Presenting Both Results
Periodic Cache Pruning

Both tables accumulate stale entries over time. Schedule a periodic WorkManager task that prunes expired rows. Run it once per day — more frequent is unnecessary; less frequent leaves stale rows consuming storage.
Gradle Dependencies
Common Pitfalls
Storing the session token unencrypted.
The
sessionToken

column is a credential. If the Room database is unencrypted, it is readable by backup extraction or USB debugging. For sensitive use cases, either encrypt the whole DB with SQLCipher or encrypt the token column value before inserting it, then decrypt it in the drain worker.
Enqueuing duplicate requests.
If the user submits the same query twice while offline, two entries are enqueued. The drain worker will process both and incur two Claude API calls. Hash the query before inserting and use
OnConflictStrategy.IGNORE
with a unique index on the hash column to prevent duplicates in
pending_ai_requests
Not pruning before fetching in the drain worker.
If you fetch without pruning first, the worker processes entries that will never produce a useful result (TTL expired, token stale). Prune at the start of
doWork()
— the SQL
WHERE
clause in
fetchLive()
filters stale entries at query time, but the
pruneExpired()
call actually deletes them so they do not accumulate.
Retrying indefinitely on session token expiry.

A 401 from the proxy means the session token has expired, not that there was a transient network error. Do not retry on HTTP 401 — remove the entry and prompt the user to log in again. Only retry on network errors (no response, DNS failure) and transient server errors (HTTP 5xx).
Treating WorkManager as real-time.

WorkManager may delay execution by minutes when the device is in Doze mode or the battery saver is active. The queued result is not instant. Design the UI to reflect this: show a "Pending — will update when online" badge rather than a spinner that never resolves.
Summary
ML Kit always answers immediately on-device; the offline queue ensures Claude queries are not silently lost when connectivity is unavailable.
Two Room tables —
pending_ai_requests
and
cached_results
— handle the queue and the response cache. Both carry TTLs to prevent stale data accumulation.
WorkManager drains the queue when connectivity is restored;
ExistingWorkPolicy.KEEP
prevents duplicate drain workers and
BackoffPolicy.EXPONENTIAL
prevents hammering the proxy on repeated failures.
HTTP 401 from the proxy means token expiry — remove the entry rather than retry. Only retry on transient network errors and HTTP 5xx responses.

Session tokens stored in the queue must be protected: use SQLCipher for sensitive apps, or encrypt the token column individually. The Claude API key never appears in the queue — it stays on the backend proxy.
